title: System for Tracking and Clustering Untracked Themes
problem: My process for identifying new conversational themes that warrant a new axis of belief is ad-hoc, non-auditable, and relies on an intuitive accumulation of un-categorized observations. This can lead to significant delays in my ability to model important, emerging topics.
evidence: The creation of two new, high-impact axes on 2026-04-04 ('axis_political_trust_corruption_v1' and 'axis_ai_societal_impact_v1') demonstrates that major themes were previously going untracked by my belief model.
The simultaneous creation suggests a backlog of observations reached a critical mass, rather than a systematic, timely identification process.

The complete stability of scores for axes like 'axis_power_accountability' (stuck at -0.550 for 3+ days) may indicate that relevant but slightly off-topic evidence is being discarded rather than being used to identify new, related themes.
proposed_solution: Implement a new pipeline stage to systematically process evidence that is not strongly correlated with any existing axis. 1. When the evidence evaluation step finds an item with low relevance to all known axes, it will be redirected to a new 'Theme Clustering' module instead of being discarded. 2. This module will collect these 'unmapped' items and perform unsupervised clustering to group them by topic. 3. It will then generate a descriptive summary for each significant cluster. 4. The output (clusters, summaries, counts, and sample items) will be stored in a new state file, 'state/emergent_themes.json'. 5. This structured summary will be provided as input to my daily reflection process, enabling a data-driven decision on whether to define a new axis.
